5.note: Sincere faith which first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice — the Deuteronomic command to transmit the faith across generations (teach them diligently to your children, Deuteronomy 6:7) finds its personal embodiment in Timothy's family; the multigenerational chain of sincere faith is the lived reality of Moses's instruction

Faith transmitted from grandmother to mother to son — the psalmist's call to tell the next generation the praiseworthy deeds of YHWH (Psalms 78:3-6: 'what our ancestors have told us... so the next generation would know them') is the theological framework for multigenerational faith transmission

6.note: The gift of God through the laying on of my hands — the ordination of Joshua by Moses (Numbers 27:18-23: 'Take Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is the spirit; lay your hand on him') is the foundational OT type for the laying-on-of-hands as a rite of commissioning for ministry succession; Timothy's ordination follows the Mosaic pattern

8.note: Do not be ashamed of the testimony... join me in suffering — the Servant who did not hide his face from shame and spitting (Isaiah 50:6) and 'set his face like flint' (Isaiah 50:7) rather than be disgraced is the OT paradigm for the unflinching witness; Paul's call not to be ashamed of chains activates the Servant's shameless suffering as the pattern for Timothy's own public witness

9.note: Called us with a holy calling... grace given to us in Christ Jesus before the ages began — YHWH's address to Israel 'I have called you by name, you are mine' (Isaiah 43:1) is the prophetic antecedent for the NT calling language; the gracious, personal, pre-temporal call grounds Paul's election theology in the Servant-Israel calling of Second Isaiah

Called before the ages began — Jeremiah's prophetic commission 'before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations' (Jeremiah 1:5) is the OT precedent for pre-temporal divine calling; Paul applies this prophetic-calling template to the entire people of God in Christ

10.note: Who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light — the eschatological promise of Isaiah 25:8 ('he will swallow up death forever; the Lord GOD will wipe away tears from all faces') is fulfilled in the appearing of Christ Jesus who has destroyed death; the Isaianic abolition of death is the OT ground for the gospel's claim

Abolished death — Hosea's ransom oracle 'I will ransom them from the power of Sheol; I will redeem them from Death' (Hosea 13:14) stands behind the death-abolition language; Paul cites this text explicitly in 1 Corinthians 15:55, and the same OT ground underlies 2 Timothy 1:10

11.note: Appointed a herald and apostle and teacher of this gospel — the herald of good news whose feet are beautiful on the mountains (Isaiah 52:7: 'how beautiful are the feet of him who brings good news') is the OT image for the gospel-proclaimer; Paul's self-designation as 'herald' (kēryx) directly echoes the Isaianic herald-figure of the new exodus

Herald of the gospel — the herald who climbs the mountain to say to the cities of Judah 'Here is your God!' (Isaiah 40:9) provides the OT commission for the apostolic herald; the herald of the new-exodus good news (Isaiah 40:9; 2 Timothy 52:7) is the prophetic antecedent for Paul's apostolic role

12.note: I know whom I have believed, and I am convinced he is able to guard what I have entrusted — YHWH's declaration 'you are my witnesses... that I am he; before me no god was formed, nor shall there be any after me' (Isaiah 43:10-11) is the OT ground for the certainty of personal knowledge of God; Paul's 'I know whom I have believed' echoes the witness-testimony language of Second Isaiah

What I have entrusted to him until that day — the psalmist's 'into your hands I commit my spirit' (Psalms 31:5, cited by Jesus on the cross, Luke 23:46) is the OT pattern for the personal deposit of trust; Paul's commitment of his life and ministry to God's keeping follows the same act of entrusting all to the faithful God

8.note: Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, the offspring of David — the Davidic promise (I will raise up your offspring after you and establish his throne forever) is fulfilled in the resurrection of David's descendant Jesus; the risen Christ is the Davidic heir whose kingdom will have no end

19.note: The Lord knows those who are his -- Korah's rebellion recalled

16.note: All Scripture is breathed out by God — the divine-origin claim for Scripture echoes YHWH's instruction to Moses to write down the Song (Deuteronomy 31:19) as a witness; the written word as YHWH's own testimony against and for Israel; now extended to all Scripture

8.note: Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness which the Lord, the righteous judge, will award — YHWH coming with his reward, his recompense before him; Paul's awaited crown from the righteous judge at the parousia echoes the Isaianic expectation of the Lord's coming with vindication for the righteous
